VIETNAM VISA

Vietnam Visa Information

  • Visa Type: E-Visa
  • Length of Stay: 30 days
  • Validity: 30 days
  • Entry: Single
  • Visa Accepted At: All Ports of Entry
  • Method: Paperless
  • Cost: 3,800/-

Vietnam Visa Requirement :-
Passport

Vietnam Visa General Information

Yes, Indian passport holders need a valid visa to enter Vietnam for tourism purposes. You can easily apply for an e-visa online without needing to visit an embassy or consulate

The Vietnam visa price for Indians is 2100 INR/- for a single entry. The visa is valid for 30 days and allows for a 30-day stay in Vietnam.

Yes, Indian citizens can apply for a multiple-entry Vietnam visa through Real Trips. A multiple-entry Vietnam e-visa is valid for 90 days and allows you to stay in the country for up to 90 days.

No, Vietnam is not visa-free for Indians. Indian passport holders need a valid visa to enter Vietnam for tourism purposes Do Indians need visa for Vietnam if they have US visa? Yes, Indians still need a tourist visa to enter Vietnam, even if they have a U.S. visa.

DUBAI (UAE) VISA

DUBAI Visa Information

  • Visa Type: E-Visa
  • Length of Stay: 30 days
  • Validity: 60 days
  • Entry: Single
  • Visa Accepted At: All Ports of Entry
  • Method: Paperless
  • Cost: 8,500/-

Dubai Visa Requirement :-
Passport

Dubai Visa General Information

Yes. Indian passport holders must have a valid visa to enter Dubai (UAE) for tourism or business purposes. You can apply for an e-visa online before travelling without visiting an embassy or consulate.

Yes, Indians are eligible for visa on arrival in Dubai . However, you must meet the following requirements to be eligible:
You must have a valid US visa or;
A valid green card issued by the US or;
A valid Schengen or EU residence visa or;
A valid UK residence visa.
The visa issued by the US, UK, or EU must have at least 6 months of validity.

Indian passport holders can apply for various types of Dubai visas, including:
Tourist visa: For leisure travel.
Here are the types of tourist visa you can apply:
a) Dubai 30 days single entry visa
b) Dubai 30 days multiple entry visa
c) Dubai 60 days single entry visa
d) Dubai 60 days multiple entry visa
e) Dubai 5 year visa 14-day business visa: For business meetings and conferences. This allows entry into the free trade zone, too.
Transit visa : For layovers in Dubai lasting up to 48 or 96 hours (depending on which one you choose).
Student visa: For pursuing education in Dubai.
Cruise visa: for those taking a cruise from Dubai

It’s recommended to apply for a Dubai visa at least 1-2 weeks before your intended travel date. This allows ample time for Dubai visa processing and addressing any potential issues with your application.

The Dubai visa price for Indians for upto 30 day stay is ₹6,250 for a single entry (excluding any service fees). The visa is valid for 60 days and permits a stay of up to 30 days in the UAE.
Visa for Dubai from India prices (excluding service fees):
30 days single entry visa: ₹6,250
60 days single entry visa: ₹11,500
60 days multiple entry visa: ₹18,850
5 year visa multiple entry visa: ₹90,000
Note: Real Trips does not charge a service fee unless your e-visa is not issued within the expected timeframe

During peak travel seasons (e.g., holidays, shopping festivals), apply for your Dubai visa at least 3-4 weeks in advance to avoid delays. Processing times may be longer during these periods due to the high volume of people travelling to Dubai.

1. Daily penalties:
Once your visa expires, you will be subject to daily fines for each day you remain in the country illegally. The fine typically starts at AED 200 for the first day of overstay and increases by AED 100 for each subsequent day.
Paying the fine: These fines must be paid before you are allowed to leave the country. You can pay the fines at immigration offices, airports, or other designated Immigration procedures centres
2. Exit process :
When you attempt to leave the UAE after overstaying, you must pay the accumulated fines at the airport's immigration counter or other designated points. Immigration officers will not allow you to depart until all fines are settled.
Detention: In some cases, particularly if the overstay period is significant, authorities may detain individuals until the fines are paid, which could result in missed flights or further complications.
3. Long-term consequences Future visa applications :
Overstaying can negatively impact your ability to obtain future visas for Dubai or other UAE Emirates. Authorities keep a record of overstays, and a history of noncompliance can lead to visa rejections.
Entry bans: In severe cases, especially if the overstay period is prolonged or if fines are not paid, you may face an entry ban. This could prevent you from re-entering the UAE for a specified period or indefinitely.
Legal consequences: In extreme cases of overstaying, legal action may be taken against you, which could include deportation or further restrictions on your ability to travel.
4. Visa extension:
Preemptive action: If you anticipate needing more time in Dubai than your visa allows, it is important to apply for a visa extension well before your current visa expires. Extensions are typically granted for an additional 30 days, but this depends on your visa type and the authorities' discretion.
Extension process: To apply for an extension, you must contact the General Directorate of Residency and Foreigners Affairs (GDRFA) or your visa sponsor. The application process can be completed online or in person, and you must pay an extension fee. In summary, overstaying your visa in Dubai can lead to significant financial penalties, legal issues, and potential bans on future travel to the UAE. To avoid these consequences, always monitor your visa's expiration date closely and take action to extend or renew it if necessary.

Yes, you can extend your stay in Dubai by applying for a visa extension. Indian citizens, like most other nationalities, have the option to extend their Dubai visa for an additional 30 days. Here’s how you can go about it:
1. Eligibility for extension:
Most types of Dubai visas, including tourist and visit visas, are eligible for extension. However, some visas, such as special entry visas or certain short-term visit visas, may not be extendable. It’s essential to check the specific terms of your visa before applying for an extension.
2. How to apply for an extension Contact your sponsor :
If your visa was obtained through an authorized sponsor (such as Real Trips or a travel agency), you must contact them to initiate the extension process. They will submit the necessary application on your behalf. General Directorate of Residency and Foreigners Affairs (GDRFA): You can apply for the extension through the GDRFA, either online via their official portal or by visiting one of their service centres in Dubai. The GDRFA handles all visa-related matters, including extensions.
3. Documents required:
Passport: Your passport must be valid for at least six months beyond the new extended visa period. f your current visa or entry permit. Current visa Proof of accommodation Extension application form: This form can be completed online or in person at the GDRFA centre. Photograph: A recent passport-sized photo may be required for the application.
4. Fees and processing time:
Extension fee: A fee is associated with extending your visa, typically around AED 1200 -1373, depending on the visa type.
Processing time: The extension process usually takes 1-3 business days, but applying well before your current visa expires is advisable.
5. Multiple extensions:
Second extension: In many cases, you can apply for a second extension, allowing you to stay in Dubai for an additional 30 days after the first extension. However, the total stay should not exceed 90 days from your initial entry.
6. Travel during the extension period:
Staying within the UAE: Once your visa is extended, you can continue to travel within Dubai and the other Emirates as usual. However, leaving the UAE during the extension period may result in the visa being voided, so it’s essential to plan accordingly Limitations: Be aware that repeatedly extending your visa without a valid reason could raise concerns with immigration authorities and may affect future visa applications
7. Grace period:
Grace period consideration: Sometimes, a grace period may be granted after your visa expires, during which you can apply for an extension without incurring fines. However, this is not always guaranteed, so applying for an extension before your visa expires is crucial.
8. What if my extension is denied?:
Alternative options: If your extension application is denied, you must leave the UAE before your current visa expires to avoid penalties. Alternatively, you can reapply for a new visa from your home country or a neighbouring country and re-enter Dubai if needed. In summary, extending your stay in Dubai is a straightforward process, provided you meet the eligibility criteria and submit your application in a timely manner. Always ensure your passport is valid, and consult with your visa sponsor or the GDRFA to navigate the extension process smoothly.

Standard Dubai tourist visas are typically single-entry, meaning you can enter Dubai once during the visa’s validity. If you need to make multiple entries, you should apply for a multipleentry visa, which allows multiple visits during its validity period.
Here are multiple entry visa information:
1. Dubai 60 days multiple entry
2. Dubai 5 years multiple entry

If your travel plans to Dubai are delayed or change after you receive your visa, you must ensure that your visa is still valid for your new travel dates.
Check validity period: Confirm the visa’s validity period (usually starting from the date of issue or intended entry) and ensure it aligns with your new travel dates.
Cancellation and reapplication: If your plans are significantly delayed, it might be best to cancel your current visa and reapply closer to your new travel date to avoid any complications.

THAILAND (TDAC) VISA

THAILAND Visa Information

  • Visa Type: TDAC
  • Length of Stay: 90 days
  • Validity: 90 days
  • Entry: Single
  • One-Click Application: Fastest Process
  • Method: Paperless
  • Cost: TBA /-*

Thailand Visa Requirement :-
Passport

Thailand Digital Arrival Card
Thailand  Visa General Information

TDAC stands for the Thailand Digital Arrival Card, a mandatory digital process that all travellers must fill before travelling to Thailand. TDAC serves as an official record of the traveller's entry.

Indian citizens do not need a visa to visit Thailand. Still, as of May 1, 2025, the Thailand Digital Arrival Card (TDAC) is mandatory for all Indian travellers entering Thailand and must be completed online before arrival.

Indians planning to work, study, or do business in Thailand must apply for the appropriate NonImmigrant visa before travelling. This requires submitting extra documentation, such as sponsorship letters, contracts, or enrollment letters (depending on the purpose of your visit) at the embassy.
Note: For urgent or ad-hoc work in Thailand under the visa-free regime (such as organizing a workshop, giving a lecture, or filming a movie), you only need to notify the registrar at an airport or local immigration office. The work must be completed within 15 days.

No, if your layover is less than 12 hours and you stay within the international transit area without clearing immigration, you do not need a transit visa. However, if your layover exceeds 12 hours or you plan to leave the transit area, you must obtain the appropriate visa or fill out the TDAC accordingly.

Failing to submit the TDAC in advance may cause delays at immigration and could result in denied entry. It’s strongly advised to complete it before you travel.

SRILANKA VISA

SRILANKA Visa Information

  • Visa Type: E-visa
  • Length of Stay: 30 days
  • Validity: 180 days
  • Entry: Single
  • One-Click Application: Fastest Process
  • Method: Paperless

SRILANKA Visa Requirement :-
Passport

Srilanka  Visa General Information

Yes, Indian citizens need an Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) to visit Sri Lanka for tourism. The ETA allows you to stay in Sri Lanka for 30 days and can be applied for online without visiting the embassy or consulate.
Note: A double-entry facility is permitted from the date of first arrival to Sri Lanka within 30 days.

No. A new ETA cannot be obtained using the same passport until the current ETA expires

Real Trips ensures the timely delivery of your ETA as promised. Choosing Real Trips as your visa partner comes with several added benefits: Streamlined application process: Real Trips simplifies the ETA application, saving you both time and effort.
User-friendly interface: The platform is intuitively designed to smooth and stress-free the entire process.
Secure and confidential: Your personal data is managed with the highest standards of security and confidentiality.

No, Indians are not eligible for a for Sri Lanka. Indian passport holders need to get an ETA before entering Sri Lanka for tourism purposes.

Yes, children under 16 years of age should have an ETA before entering Sri Lanka.

MALAYASIA (MDAC) VISA

MALAYASIA ( MDAC ) Visa Information

  • Visa Type: MDAC
  • Length of Stay: 14 days
  • Validity: 30 days
  • Entry: Single
  • One-Click Application: Fastest Process
  • Method: Paperless
  • Cost: TBA /-*

Malaysia Visa Information:-
Passport

Malaysia ( MDAC ) Visa General Information

No, Indians don't need a visa to visit Malaysia for up to 30 days. However,Indian visitors to Malaysia need a mandatory Malaysia Digital Arrival Card (MDAC) - the Department of Immigration issues this permit, which can be processed online.

The visa-free policy allows Indian passport holders to stay in Malaysia for up to 30 days. The MDAC will be valid for a single entry, allowing a 30-day stay in the country.

Transit visa for Malaysia:
The Malaysia transit visa from India is used for travellers who need to transit for more than 24 hours or plan to leave the transit area.
Student visa for Malaysia:
Students from India can also apply for a study visa for Malaysia.This is a long-term visa allowing you to study in the country.
Malaysia permanent residence visa:
Indians looking to stay in Malaysia for the long term must apply for a permanent residency visa. This will eventually allow Indians to get Malaysian citizenship.
Malaysia employment visa:
Travellers looking to work in Malaysia can apply for an employment visa. This will allow them to be employed and live in Malaysia.

Real Trips guarantees the timely delivery of visas as promised. Additional benefits of choosing Real Trips as your visa partner include:
Streamlined visa application:
Real Trips simplifies the visa application process, saving you time and effort.
User-friendly platform:
The platform is designed for ease of use, making the application process straightforward and hassle-free.
Secure handling:
Your personal information is handled with the utmost security and confidentiality.

INDONESIA VISA

INDONESIA Visa Information

  • Visa Type: E- Visa
  • Length of Stay: 30 days
  • Validity: 90 days
  • Entry: Single
  • One-Click Application: Fastest Process
  • Method: Paperless
  • Cost: 4500/-*

Indonesia E-Visa Requirement:-
Passport

Indonesia Visa General Information

Yes Indian passport holders need a visa to enter Indonesia for tourism purposes, to visit family and friends, to attend meetings, incentives, conventions, and exhibitions as an attendee and to transit in Indonesia to other countries.
Since Indonesia does not offer visa-free entry to Indians, you can easily apply for an eVOA before entering the country, offering a more convenient way.

The Indonesia visa fee for Indian citizens is for a single entry (excluding any service fees). The tourist visa is valid for 90 days and allows for a 30-day stay in Indonesia.
Note: Real Trips does not charge a service fee unless your e-visa is not issued within the expected timeframe.

The validity period of your Indonesia visa refers to the timeframe during which you can enter the country. This period is indicated by the "must be used by" date on your e-visa. The length of stay, on the other hand, is the number of days you are permitted to remain in Indonesia after entering the country, as specified by the "Duration of Stay" on your visa. ,br> Detailed explanation:
Validity Period:
The validity period is the window within which you must enter Indonesia. For example, if your visa is valid from January 1 to March 31, you must enter Indonesia on or before March 31.
Length of Stay:
The length of stay is the maximum number of days you can remain in Indonesia after entry. For instance, if your visa allows for a 30-day stay, you must leave the country within 30 days of your arrival, regardless of when you entered during the validity period.
Tip:
Plan your travel to ensure that your entry into Indonesia falls within the visa’s validity period and that you adhere to the permitted length of stay to avoid any immigration issues.

Yes, Indian citizens can apply for a (also called the Single Entry Visit Visa), which is suitable for longer stays for tourism, cultural, or family visits. You can easily apply online through the official website or Real Trips.

Yes, you can apply. Please make sure you provide your name in both the First Name and Last Name fields. The information provided in e-VOA must match the details present in your passport.

Yes, children or infants need an e-visa to enter Indonesia. They need to fill out the separate e-visa application.

No, you cannot use a new passport to enter Indonesia if you applied using an old passport. You need to reapply using a new passport. You cannot enter with an old passport. Immigration authorities in Indonesia or Bali do not grant entry to any tourist whose passport is less than valid.

No, Indonesia is not visa-free for Indians.Indian passport holders need a visa to enter Indonesia for tourism purposes.

AZERBAIJAN VISA

Azerbaijan Visa Requirement:-
Passport

Azerbaijan Visa General Information

Yes , Indian passport holders need a valid visa to travel to the Republic of Azerbaijan (or Baku) for tourism purposes. Indian travellers can apply for an e-visa, which is a convenient and efficient way to obtain the necessary travel authorisation without visiting an embassy or consulate.

Yes, Real Trips offers an express e-visa service, which allows applicants to receive their visa within 24 hours of application submission. This service is particularly useful for travellers who need a visa on short notice.

Yes, the Azerbaijan e-visa can be used for transit purposes if you need to pass through Azerbaijan en route to another destination. However, the transit should be completed within the allowed 30-day stay period.

The Azerbaijan visa fee for Indian citizens is ₹2,272 for a single entry (excluding any service fees). The is valid for 90 days and allows for a 30-day stay in Azerbaijan.
Note: Real Trips does not charge a service fee unless your e-visa is not issued within the expected timeframe.

No, Azerbaijan is not visa-free for Indians. Indians need to get a tourist visa entering Azerbaijan.

No, you don't need Azerbaijan if you are not leaving the international transit area.

Yes, children/minors need a separate application for the Azerbaijan e-visa.

EGYPT VISA

Egypt Visa Information

  • Visa Type: E- Visa
  • Length of Stay: 30 days
  • Validity: 90 days
  • Entry: Single
  • One-Click Application: Fastest Process
  • Method: Paperless
  • Cost: 4,000/-*

Egypt Visa Requirement:-
Passport

Egypt Visa General Information

Yes, Indians must have a valid visa to visit Egypt for tourism purposes. You can apply for an Egypt e-visa online before travelling without visiting the Egyptian embassy or consulate. Additionally, Indian travellers need an “Authorised Travel Agent Letter of Invitation” to enter the country.Real Trips is an authorized travel agent, and each e-visa applied on Real Trips will come with an “Authorized Travel Agent Letter of Invitation.”

Yes, all travellers, including children and minors, are required to have a valid Egypt e-visa. Each child must have an individual visa, regardless of age.

With an Egypt e-visa, Indian citizens can stay in Egypt for up to 30 days per entry. The e-visa is typically valid for 90 days from the date of issue, within which you can enter the country.
Note: Make sure to exit Egypt before your 30-day stay period expires to avoid overstaying penalties.

Yes, there is a difference:
Single-entry e-visa:
Allows you to enter Egypt only once within the validity period. If you leave the country, you will need to apply for a new visa to re-enter.
Multiple-entry e-visa:
Allows you to enter and exit Egypt multiple times within the validity period of the visa. This is useful for travellers who plan to visit neighbouring countries and return to Egypt.
Planning to visit Egypt multiple times? Easily apply for an Egypt multiple-entry visa with Real Trips and avoid the hassle of reapplying!

Indians can apply for different types of Egypt visas depending on the purpose of their visit:
Tourist visa:
For sightseeing and leisure activities.
Business visa:
For attending meetings, conferences, or conducting business-related activities.
Transit visa:
for travellers passing through Egypt to another destination.
Student visa:
For individuals pursuing education in Egypt.
Note: Ensure you apply for the correct visa type based on your travel purpose to avoid complications during your trip. Real Trips only provides a tourist visa

The Egypt visa fees for Indian citizens are 2,100 for a single entry (excluding any service fees).
The visa is valid for 90 days and permits a stay of up to 30 days in Egypt
Note: Real Trips does not charge a service fee unless your e-visa is not issued within the expected timeframe.

Yes, children/minors need a separate application for the Azerbaijan e-visa.

GEORGIA VISA

Georgia Visa Information

  • Visa Type: E- Visa
  • Length of Stay: 30 days
  • Validity: 120 days
  • Entry: Multipal
  • Method: Paperless
  • Cost: TBA /-*

Georgia Visa Requirement:-

1. Passport 2. Bank Statement 3. Photo

Georgia Visa General Information

IYes, ndians need a valid visa to visit Georgia for tourism purposes. Indians are eligible to apply for an online without visiting a Georgian embassy or consular office. You will get a short- term multiple-entry visa which is valid for 120 days, and allows you to stay up to 30 days.
Note: Indians are not eligible for a for Georgia.

The Georgia visa fees for Indians is ₹3000 for a multiple-entry visa. The tourist visa is valid for 120 days and allows for a 30-day stay in Georgia.
Note: Real Trips does not charge a service fee unless your e-visa is not issued within the expected timeframe.

Yes, Indians holding a valid visa or residents of the following countries are visa-free for Georgia and may stay for a maximum of 90 days within a 180-day period. US
UK
Canada
South Korea
Australia
New Zealand
Israel
Japan
Schengen countries
Valid residency of GCC countries
Note: If you don't have a valid visa or a resident visa from the above countries, you need to get an e-visa.

Yes, Children/minors need a separate Georgia e-visa. When submitting an application for children, the details of the legal representative are to be provided.

Once the Georgia e-visa application is submitted, it cannot be updated. You need to get a new e-visa.

AUSTRALIA VISA

Australia Visa Information

  • Visa Type: E- Visa
  • Length of Stay: 90 days
  • Validity: 03 Years
  • Entry: Multipal
  • Method: Paperless
  • Cost: TBA /-*

Australia Visa Requirement:-

1. Passport 2. Bank Statement 3. Photo 4. Income Tax Return (ITR)

Australia Visa General Information

Yes, Indian passport holders need a visa to travel to Australia. You can apply for a visitor visa (subclass 600), which is issued for short-term tourism, business, or family visits.

No, Australia now issues visas that are electronically linked to the passport number. When your visa is approved, you will receive an e-visa PDF via mail. Australia has now moved away from traditional visa stamps; hence, you will not receive a physical visa stamp or label in your passport.

No, Australia does not offer Indians a visa upon arrival. They must obtain a visa before travelling to the country. A visitor visa (subclass 600) is the most suitable option for short-term visits related to tourism or business purposes.

A multiple-entry visa allows you to enter Australia more than once (multiple times) within the visa's validity period.
For example, you have a multiple-entry Australia visa with 12 months' validity (January 1, 2024, to December 31, 2024), which allows a stay of 3 months per visit (but not more than 6 months in total within those 12 months). This means you can visit Australia: For the first time on January 1, 2024, and stay until March 31, 2024 (3 months). For the second time on July 1, 2024, and stay until September 30, 2024 (3 months). All you need to make sure is that (within the time frame of your visa's validity period) you do not stay for more than 3 months during each visit.

Although there are many types of Australian visas available for foreign nationals, here are the common ones:
Visitor visa (subclass 600):
Issued for short-term visits for tourism or business purposes and generally allows a stay of 3 months.
Transit visa (subclass 771): This is issued for transit purposes and allows you to have a layover of up to 72 hours.
Student visa (subclass 500):
This allows foreign nationals to study and work (conditions apply) in Australia. It is valid for a maximum of five years and allows you to travel in and out of the country multiple times during this period. Parent visa (subclass 103): Allows parents of settled Australian citizens, permanent residents, or eligible New Zealand citizens to live in Australia permanently.
Temporary skill shortage visa (subclass 482):
This visa enables an employer to sponsor a qualified worker with the necessary skills to fill a position. The work visa can be valid for 2 - 4 years, depending on the stream you apply for.
Partner visa (subclass 820/801):
The partner visa allows eligible citizens or residents to live in Australia. This visa is initially temporary (subclass 820), but after 2 years, you can submit your application for permanent residency (subclass 801).

JAPAN VISA

Japan Visa Information

  • Visa Type: Sticker
  • Length of Stay: 30 days
  • Validity: 90 days
  • Entry: Multipal
  • Cost: TBA /-*

Japan Visa General Information

Yes, Indian citizens need a valid visa to visit Japan, whether for tourism, business, or social purposes. You must obtain a visa before travelling. It's a good idea to apply well in advance to ensure sufficient processing time for the visa before your trip.

No, Japan does not offer a visa-on-arrival facility for Indians. Therefore, planning and applying in advance is crucial to ensure you have all the required documentation ready for your trip.

Our ETA includes shipping your passport from your location to our Fulfillment centre, getting your visa done in the embassy, and getting back to you! Our ETA typically ranges between 20 and 25 days for this entire journey. Fun fact: We determine your ETA the moment you enter your pin code using our consulate detector tool. Just insert your current pin code in the check serviceability section!

China Visa Requirement:-

1. Passport 2. Bank Statement 3. Photo 4. Income Tax Return (ITR)

Georgia Visa General Information

Yes, Indians need a valid visa to visit China for tourism purposes. China offers a tourist visa called an L visa, and Indians cannot get a visa on arrival. The visa is valid for 90 days, and the length of stay is 30 days.

At , your documents are encrypted multiple times, making them inaccessible to anyone else. We do not sell your data to third parties. Additionally, we employ a highly reliable courier service for a secure collection and delivery of your documents, guaranteeing their safety at every step.

The visa processing procedure is the same for a child who is an independent applicant or an accompanying applicant sharing the same passport as the principal applicant. Therefore, the child will incur the same service fee. To apply for the Indian-to-China visa, you will need to provide additionally:
Birth certificate.
Parents' identity document proof such as passport or residence permit.

For residents of Maharashtra and Karnataka, does not provide China visa services because applicants from these states are required to submit their visa applications in person at the Consulate General of China in Mumbai. The Chinese consulate sets this requirement and necessitates personal attendance.

We partner with reliable and well-known delivery services to ensure that your documents are handled with the utmost care and security.
We understand the concerns that come with sending important documents like passports, so we provide regular notifications throughout the delivery process, keeping you informed every step of the way. Rest assured, we take every precaution to protect your personal information, and your documents are always in safe hands with .

At , we determine your jurisdiction the moment you enter your pin code using our detector tool. Just insert your pin code in the check serviceability section, and we will do the rest!
But just to make you an expert in China visas, your jurisdiction depends on two factors: the Address on the back of your passport and your current address. If your current address is in a different from your passport address, you need to provide proof of address (rent agreement/utility bill (electricity/gas) for your current address as well.
China Visa Application Centre - New Delhi Andhra Pradesh, Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Goa, Gujarat, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, Kerala, Ladakh, Madhya Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Punjab, Rajasthan, Sikkim, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Tripura, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand.

China Visa Application Centre - Kolkata. Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, Chhattisgarh.
